description: Close or re-scope beaverGame #29 (Babylon migration umbrella). All sprint PRs (#32, #37, #38, #39) are merged — post a completion summary on the issue and explicitly re-file BG-003 (snap CI), BG-005 (Poisson scatter), BG-001 (HDRI), BG-015 (material verification) as Phase 1 tracking items. This is the 10-minute action that makes 'migration complete' mean something on the roadmap.

Resolve the :3035 port collision and run the first end-to-end test of the asset-foundry Frame remote in the same session. Open frame-dev.sh and the shell remoteBase map, assign gcgcca a distinct port, update both files in a single commit (closes act-2026-04-30-roadmap-640008), then run frame-dev.sh and confirm asset-foundry loads at :3035 without a federation error. Also verify shell PR #76 disposition — if it is still open after dbce227 landed to main directly, close it.

Diagnose the 46-suggested / 0-followed funnel from Monday's sessions before ADR-0068 is treated as enforceable. Read ~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl and classify at least 5 suggestion entries by reason-for-ignore: wrong context, wrong moment in session flow, genuinely ignorable, or agent non-compliance. Separately, confirm whether PR-comment marker credits are being counted as real-time skill invocations in the audit — if they are, the telemetry model needs correction before the compliance rule compounds further on synthetic data.

Write ADR-0067 for the ojfbot/github-actions shared composite action. The document does not currently exist but is cited in multiple PRs and in this article's Decisions section. Cover: the DRY extraction rationale, the zero-width PR window bug and fix, the PR-comment marker credit mechanism, the fleet adoption model (6 of N active repos on day one), and the branch-referencing pattern for safely testing action changes before rolling to @v1 consumers (`ojfbot/github-actions/.github/actions/skill-audit@your-branch`).

Audit the :3035 port collision between asset-foundry and gcgcca. Open frame-dev.sh and shell's remoteBase map side by side, assign a distinct port to each remote, and update both files in a single PR. Shell #76 must not merge until this is resolved — merging it as-is bakes the collision into the host config. The fix is mechanical: pick a free port for asset-foundry (e.g. :3036), update frame-dev.sh and remoteBase in one commit, then amend #76 or open a follow-on PR.

Disposition beaverGame #27 (Three.js playability pass). Open the PR and decide: merge-as-spec or close-as-superseded. If closing, extract the heightAt(x,z) bilinear-blend fix into #28's behavioral-spec checklist before deleting the branch — that logic is the terrain-sampling spec the Babylon migration must satisfy. Leave a comment in #28 linking to the decision. This takes under 15 minutes and is the single action that unblocks Babylon bootstrap.

Begin the Pocock skills adoption triage with core #81 only. #81 (foundation: /grill-with-docs, CONTEXT.md, agent defaults) is the prerequisite for all subsequent phases (#82–#85). Review and merge #81 before opening #82 for review. Merging out of order produces skill catalog phase-registry inconsistencies where later commands reference skill entries that do not yet exist. Do not batch-review all five — proceed one PR at a time in phase order.

Merge core #94 (game-engine architecture spike — Babylon.js recommended). This is the decision record that justifies discarding beaverGame's entire Three.js codebase. Draft PR #28 is already open; without #94 merged, the rationale exists only in a PR description and will be re-litigated in future sessions.

Name a disposition for beaverGame #27 (Three.js playability pass) before #28 advances past the Babylon bootstrap. The heightAt(x,z) bilinear-blend fix is a correctness issue — leaving #27 open creates ambiguity about what the snap-rig oracle actually certifies. Merge it as spec, or close it as superseded. Do not leave it in an undecided state.

Merge shell #76 (register asset_foundry MF remote at :3035). One-line host config addition, zero risk surface, prerequisite for the asset-foundry Frame MF front door being visible in the dev environment. Can close in under ten minutes and unblocks the asset-foundry platform roadmap.

Write Vitest tests for beaverGame's load-glb.ts validation tripwire covering all four rejection paths: missing validation.json, rejected status field, malformed JSON, and schema mismatch. The tripwire is the gate that prevents a malformed or unvalidated .glb from being loaded into the scene — if it passes something it shouldn't, a corrupted or oversized asset silently enters the render pipeline. This is BG-004 and the highest-leverage untested code in the loader. The four paths map directly to the four branches in the validation check function.

Add the snap-and-diff CI step to beaverGame's .github/workflows/ci.yml. The snap step runs pnpm tsx scripts/snap.ts to render a headless screenshot of the full scene in its default initial state (terrain loaded, no player actions taken), uploads the result as a workflow artifact, and diffs it against the committed baseline at tests/baselines/scene-snap.png using SSIM ≥ 0.98 as the pass threshold. On the first run, if no baseline exists, the step should write the baseline and pass — subsequent runs enforce the diff. This is BG-003 and the prerequisite for visual regression as a CI gate.

Verify the asset-foundry → beaverGame asset sync end-to-end: with both repos checked out as siblings (../beaverGame and ../asset-foundry), run pnpm gen-asset birch_sapling in asset-foundry and confirm that birch_sapling.glb and birch_sapling.validation.json land in beaverGame/public/assets/. Document the expected sibling-directory layout in asset-foundry's CLAUDE.md. This is the manual precursor to AF-013 CI automation and currently the highest-risk undocumented path in the ecosystem — the Phase 0 spike was developed in isolation and this sync has never been verified against a real beaverGame checkout.

Verify conflict state of daily-logger daily-cleaner PRs #155, #157, #159, #161 (opened April 14–17) before touching #163–#169. Open each PR in the GitHub UI, check for conflict markers, and manually resolve any conflicts on the affected branches. Once the oldest four are clean, run act-2026-04-21-sweep-914db8 to batch-merge all eight. Do not invoke the batch-merge action until conflict state is verified — the queue is 8 PRs deep and a single conflicted branch will block the entire batch.

Batch-merge daily-cleaner PRs #155, #157, #159, #161, #163, #165, #167 in sequence — rebase any that have conflicts, verify Opus-validation comment is present on each, and merge. The queue is 7 PRs deep and growing a conflict risk with each passing day.

Triage the 19 open actions: explicitly defer any action older than 14 days that has not been touched (act-2026-04-04-roadmap-e9a078, act-2026-04-07-adr-daa2ca, act-2026-04-07-roadmap-6f4599) — either close with a deferral note or assign a target date. An action board with 19 items is a backlog, not a queue.

End-to-end test the suggestion-ignored telemetry: trigger a skill suggestion in a dev session, decline it, and verify an `ignored` disposition entry appears in `~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl` with the correct skill ID and timestamp. Confirm the `followed` path still fires on invocation. This is the first full-loop verification of the completed feedback system.

Audit the approximately 16 open actions generated before commit `9c17d7f` for ID correctness: re-run the content hash (command + description + repo + date, whitespace-normalized) against each stored ID. Any mismatch means the action will not close in the next closure pipeline run. Remediate by re-generating the ID and updating the action file, or manually verifying the action is still open and re-writing with the correct hash. Complete before the next sprint closure run.

Write two ADRs in one session: (1) the `suggested_after` / `layer_affinity` catalog governance ADR (`act-2026-04-13-adr-2d947c`) — all prerequisites are now in place; (2) the content-hash action ID scheme ADR — this foundational decision just touched the entire closure pipeline and has no written rationale. Document the hash function, collision surface, and the intentional behavior when action content changes before resolution.

Confirm suggest-skills.mjs correctly matches suggested_after catalog entries against Sunday's hook invocations. Read ~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl (first clean data after ADR-0037 fix), cross-reference skill IDs against catalog suggested_after fields, verify no suggestions fire for skills with no catalog entry, and confirm no-match produces a silent no-op rather than a throw.

Write the ADR for the suggested_after and layer_affinity fields added to the skill catalog on Sunday. Document: field definitions, how suggest-skills.mjs reads them (lookup direction), runtime behavior on no-match, layer_affinity filter semantics, relationship to shell's App → Instance → Thread model, and the catalog governance model (who can write to it). This is the decision record for the forward half of the telemetry feedback loop.

Review purefoy #39 (GPU transcription observability / ProgressReporter). Validate the 8-stage progress.json schema, confirm ProgressReporter does not block the transcription hot path, and merge. PR has been open since at least 2026-04-11 and is merge-pending-review with no named reviewer.

Run quality gates against the four core telemetry commits (9e4449f, ab561c8, a7caa41, 13c4977) — suggest-skill, log-skill, and pr-skill-audit. Saturday's session showed 0% quality coverage on the code that now instruments all other skill usage. Do this before the investigation — the investigation tooling is untrusted until this passes.

Run /validate on shell's /api/beads filesystem fallback to unblock Gas Town Sprint 1. This is not blocked by MrPlug #28 and has no active owner. It is the first gate before the core-reader bead consumption scaffold can proceed.

Read ~/.claude/suggestion-log.jsonl for Saturday's two suggestion entries. Confirm the skill IDs exist in the catalog (expected: yes, already verified). Then evaluate whether the suggestion timing was reasonable — were they surfaced mid-flow when action wasn't possible? This is a product question about suggestion UX, not a bug hunt.

Verify shell's Dolt-first /api/beads aggregation falls back correctly to filesystem bead files when Dolt is unavailable. Start the shell dev server, hit the endpoint, confirm it returns beads from at least two sub-apps using the filesystem path at {repo}/.beads/{session-id}/{event-type}.json, and document the bead file location convention in shell's README. This is a prerequisite for the core-reader scaffold action.

Write the fleet-wide GET /api/beads ADR. ADR-0016 already exists and governs the per-app projection pattern. This new ADR documents the fleet extension: the FrameBeadLike type (defined in core/packages/types/src/bead.ts), the app-specific prefix convention (pure-, blog-, trip-, seh-, lean-), the empty-return-until-storage pattern, the shell Mayor aggregation strategy, and the Dolt migration path for filesystem beads. Eight repos implement this contract today without a durable decision record.

Scaffold the bead consumption view in core-reader after /validate confirms the filesystem fallback path works. Read aggregated beads from shell's /api/beads endpoint, render a raw timeline list in one of core-reader's existing tabs. Use the filesystem-fallback path — do not wait for Dolt. This is the first visible Gas Town UI and the proof that the Mayor aggregation layer works end-to-end.

Confirm the Vite alias for @ojfbot/frame-ui-components resolves from source correctly during local development after the npm migration — run the shell dev server, import a component from frame-ui-components, and verify hot-reload reflects source edits without a publish step.

Run the Storybook composition dashboard (shell #66) after the npm migration to confirm all fleet repos' component stories still resolve correctly — the file: → npm change may break story resolution if the Storybook config references local paths.

cv-builder BioDashboard is now 373 lines after the decomposition — still above the container-presenter 100-line orchestrator target. Identify the next extraction candidates and open a follow-up decomposition PR.

Add an explicit wait-for-sentinel pattern to the Playwright screenshot test and confirm it survives a cold Module Federation boot — i.e., clear the Vite cache and re-run the capture script end-to-end to verify the fix holds under slow-network conditions.

Write ADR for the Pydantic → OpenAPI → TypeScript codegen pattern established in gcgcca PRs #1 and #2. Document the invocation (python scripts/generate_schema.py, venv required, output to src/generated/types.ts), the known failure mode (custom validators and Union discriminators may not map cleanly to OpenAPI), and the purefoy CLI+UI reference. This pattern will apply to every future Python-backed Frame sub-app.

Register gcgcca as a live Module Federation remote in the shell host config at :3035. Wire it into frame-dev.sh and confirm hot-reload works in the dev environment. Type bridge is complete, MF scaffold exists — this is the milestone that makes gcgcca visible inside the Frame shell for the first time.
Scope the Hailstone migration. Define what moves, what gets rebuilt, and the target architecture inside the portfolio. Produce a short migration plan before any code is written.
